title: Houses for Rent in Euclid, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Euclid, Ohio
metaDescription: Searching for houses for rent in Euclid, Ohio? See rent ranges, transit, and housing stock for this Lake Erie suburb bordering Cleveland's Collinwood area.
answerBox: Euclid, Ohio is a Lake Erie suburb directly northeast of Cleveland's Collinwood neighborhood, spanning ZIP codes including 44117 and 44123, with a mostly mid-20th-century housing stock. Greater Cleveland rents generally run $700–$1,800/month. Tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll compare options here and in nearby Cleveland neighborhoods we manage.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Euclid, Ohio
body: Euclid sits on Lake Erie directly northeast of Cleveland's Collinwood-Nottingham area, spanning ZIP codes that include **44117 and 44123**. Much of the housing stock is mid-20th-century single-family and two-family homes, built up alongside the neighborhood's lakefront parks and its industrial corridor along the rail lines and I-90.

**We manage 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes with our strongest concentration in Collinwood (ZIP 44110), which borders Euclid directly to the south — but Euclid itself is not currently part of our managed portfolio.** If a home just across the border in Collinwood would work for you, [tell us your bedroom count and budget](/p/book-showing) and we'll show you what's currently available there.

Because Euclid sits at the far northeast edge of Cuyahoga County, it's also worth knowing the region is a snow belt: Cleveland's east side and the areas closer to the Lake/Cuyahoga County line typically see more lake-effect snowfall than west-side communities, with the heaviest totals generally between November and January while Lake Erie is still unfrozen. That's a practical factor to weigh alongside rent when comparing an east-side suburb like Euclid to a west-side one.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent in the Cleveland area?
body: Euclid's own rents vary by block and home type, but as a Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a metro median asking rent of **$1,250/month**, with 2-bedroom units around **$1,100** and 3-bedroom units around **$1,350**. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, weighted toward larger professionally-managed apartment buildings, shows a higher metro average of **$1,564/month** for roughly 787 square feet.

Euclid runs along the Lake Erie shoreline directly northeast of Cleveland's Collinwood-Nottingham neighborhood, with I-90 running through the city and providing a direct route into downtown Cleveland to the southwest. Greater Cleveland RTA bus routes connect Euclid to downtown along Euclid Avenue and Lakeshore Boulevard, and the city maintains lakefront parkland along its northern edge. For renters commuting to the region's largest employers, Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals — the two biggest employers in the county — sit closer to the University Circle area southwest of Euclid, reachable via the same Euclid Avenue corridor the RTA HealthLine runs on further into the city.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus routes on Euclid Ave and Lakeshore Blvd; I-90 access
**Location:** Lake Erie suburb directly northeast of Cleveland's Collinwood area
**Housing stock:** Mid-20th-century single-family and two-family homes

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher holders looking in Euclid
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, so acceptance in Euclid depends on the individual property owner rather than a citywide mandate. What we can confirm about our own homes: **every property Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, and our closest concentration is right next door in Collinwood (ZIP 44110). The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the countywide Housing Choice Voucher program and accepts preliminary applications year-round; our [Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) covers how the process generally works.

If your search includes both Euclid and Cleveland proper, check our [Collinwood neighborhood guide](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-collinwood-cleveland/) for homes just across the border, or browse the [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page for the broader east side. You can also compare nearby [Richmond Heights](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-richmond-heights-ohio/) and [Willowick](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-willowick-ohio/), or browse the full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/).

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses in Euclid, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently, but our strongest concentration of voucher-friendly rental homes is in Collinwood, which borders Euclid directly to the south. Tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll show you what's available there.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Euclid, Ohio?
faq.a: Euclid-specific pricing varies by block and home type, but as a Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a metro median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units, while RentCafe's July 2026 report runs higher for larger managed apartment buildings.

faq.q: What ZIP codes cover Euclid, Ohio?
faq.a: Euclid spans ZIP codes 44117 and 44123, along Lake Erie directly northeast of Cleveland's Collinwood-Nottingham neighborhood.

faq.q: Do Euclid landlords accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord, since Ohio has no statewide requirement to accept vouchers. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, with our closest concentration in neighboring Collinwood. CMHA runs the countywide voucher program.

faq.q: Does Euclid get more snow than the rest of Cleveland?
faq.a: Generally, yes. Euclid sits toward the eastern side of Cuyahoga County, and the eastern half of the county tends to see more lake-effect snow than west-side communities, with the heaviest season running roughly November through January. It's a geographic pattern tied to position relative to Lake Erie, not a comment on any specific neighborhood's desirability.
